name: visual-reasoning-robots

paperTitle: Lightweight Visual Reasoning for Socially-Aware Robots
content:
## Overview

This skill implements the technique from "Lightweight Visual Reasoning for Socially-Aware Robots".

Key contribution: Robots operating in shared human environments must not only navigate, interact, and detect their surroundings, they must also interpret and respond to dynamic, and often unpredictable, human behaviours. Although recent advances have shown promise in enhancing robotic perception and instruction-following using Vision-Language Models (VLMs), they remain limited in addressing the complexities of multimodal human-robot interactions (HRI).

## When to use

- Apply when solving lightweight problems
- When baseline approaches are insufficient
- Working with structured problem spaces

## When NOT to use

- If problem constraints limit complexity
- When simpler methods achieve target performance
- Time-critical scenarios with strict latency needs

## Core Technique

The paper proposes an approach through:

1. Problem formulation from the paper
2. Algorithm implementation as described
3. Integration with existing systems
4. Evaluation against baselines

## Implementation

1. Understand problem requirements
2. Implement core algorithm components
3. Configure evaluation metrics from paper
4. Compare against baseline methods
5. Tune hyperparameters for your domain

## References

Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.03942
